title: Ozoroa pulcherrima
intro: Ozoroa pulcherrima, also known as the Golden Leafed Milk Bush, is a shrub-like plant native to southern Africa, characterized by its attractive golden leaves and showy white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa pulcherrima include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ozoroa pulcherrima is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in the form of a tincture, to harness its potential health benefits and alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ozoroa pulcherrima is classified in the family Anacardiaceae. This family is commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family.
